headline: Scholarship Distribution Favors 3 States Disproportionately
context: JPA and MARA scholarship data for 2025 shows Selangor, Johor, and KL receive 52% of all awards despite being 38% of population.

cards.big: 52% of JPA scholarships went to 3 states that hold 38% of Malaysia's population
cards.sub: East Malaysian states received only 14% of total scholarship allocations

cards.big: JPA awarded 2,400 overseas scholarships in 2025 worth RM1.8 billion total
cards.sub: Average per-scholar cost exceeds RM750,000 for a 4-year degree abroad

cards.big: Sarawak students make up 9.2% of SPM top scorers but receive 5.1% of scholarships
cards.sub: Geographic bias in selection panels may contribute to imbalanced outcomes

cards.big: Need-based criteria account for only 20% of JPA scholarship scoring since 2018
cards.sub: Academic merit dominates but access to quality schooling is itself unequal

cards.big: Equal opportunity means adjusting for unequal starting points not just rewarding top scores
Should scholarship quotas be allocated proportionally by state population
